Harmonized Name:
Ratio of Forced Expiratory Volume in 1 second to Forced Vital Capacity
- UK Variable: FEV1/FVC
- Source Label / Description: The ratio of the volume of air that can be forcibly blown out in one second, after full inspiration, to the total volume of air that can be forcibly blown out after full inspiration.
- Target Concept: FEV1/FVC ratio
- Standard Vocabulary: LOINC
- Standard Concept Code: 19926-5
- Match Confidence: High
- Summary: The UK variable 'FEV1/FVC' maps directly to the LOINC standard concept 'FEV1/FVC ratio' with a high level of confidence. This is a key measure in pulmonary function tests, often used in the diagnosis and monitoring of conditions such as asthma.
